conversation.content:
base) Lexuss-MacBook-Pro:snatched chenlingzhi$ npm start

> snatched@1.0.0 start
> node server.js

node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1247
  throw err;
  ^

Error: Cannot find module '/Users/chenlingzhi/Desktop/snatched/server.js'
    at Function._resolveFilename (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1244:15)
    at Function._load (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1070:27)
    at TracingChannel.traceSync (node:diagnostics_channel:322:14)
    at wrapModuleLoad (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:217:24)
    at Function.executeUserEntryPoint [as runMain] (node:internal/modules/run_main:170:5)
    at node:internal/main/run_main_module:36:49 {
  code: 'MODULE_NOT_FOUND',
  requireStack: []
}

Node.js v22.13.1
(base) Lexuss-MacBook-Pro:snatched chenlingzhi$

This error means that Node can’t find the file `server.js` in the directory where it expects it. In other words, you have an npm start script that runs `node server.js`, but the file does not exist at `/Users/chenlingzhi/Desktop/snatched/server.js`.

How to fix:
1. Check if `server.js` exists in the project root.
2. If it is in another folder (like `src/`), update package.json start script.
3. Make sure filename matches exactly (`server.js` vs `Server.js`).
4. Ensure package.json start script matches the actual entry file.
5. If needed, run `npm install` for dependencies.
